commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Cantar Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Cantar's diesel engines are commercial workhorses, but the 4D56 timing chain and 4M40 EGR cooler failures dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific Cantar variant.

problems.h4: 4D56 Timing Chain Failure - Why Early Cantar Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Original timing chain tensioner design allows excessive slack, causing chain jump and valve timing loss under load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, chain before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; not suitable post-failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Head removal, valve inspection, gasket replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Only if valves undamaged; risk of hidden cracks

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4D56 with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for post-failure; addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: On a Cantar worth £3,000-£8,000, a preventative chain kit at 50k miles is cost-effective. Once the chain has jumped, replacement with a rebuilt unit including the upgraded tensioner is the only reliable solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4D56 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Cantar

problems.h4: 4M40 EGR Cooler Failure - Cracked Cooler Drives Cantar Replacement
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler internal cracking allows coolant into exhaust, causing white smoke, overheating, and eventual head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove/blank EGR valve, replace cooler assembly
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; doesn't address head stress

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, machine surface, new gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if head undamaged; risk of warping

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4M40 with EGR delete option, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for post-failure vehicles

problems.recommendation: If your Cantar shows white smoke or coolant loss, act immediately. For vehicles over 70k miles, a rebuilt engine with EGR system modification offers better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4M40 Mitsubishi Cantar engine replacement

problems.h4: 4M42 Fuel Pipe Recall Leak - 2007-2010 Cantar Safety Issue
problems.rootCause: Factory fuel supply pipe prone to cracking under vibration, causing diesel leaks, fire risk, and potential engine starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty fuel pipe with revised part (if recall not completed)
repairOptions.longevity: Essential safety fix; does not address other failures
Addresses symptoms; not root cause if pipe failed

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4M42 with updated fuel system components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vehicles or post-leak damage

problems.recommendation: Check if your Cantar's recall was completed. If the fuel pipe leaked and caused injection system damage, replacement with a rebuilt unit including updated components is more economical than piecemeal repairs.
problems.cta: Compare 4M42 Mitsubishi Cantar engine prices now

problems.h4: 4P10 Common Rail Injector Failure - Modern Cantar Diagnostic Challenge
problems.rootCause: High-pressure common rail injectors suffer internal wear, causing poor atomisation, DPF clogging, and loss of power.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace one faulty injector, code to ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary; other injectors likely to follow

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all injectors, rail pressure test, ECU calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine otherwise healthy; high labour cost

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4P10 with upgraded injectors, DPF-friendly mapping, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or DPF-damaged vehicles

problems.recommendation: On Euro 6 Cantars with DPF systems, injector failure often cascades into expensive DPF damage. For vehicles over 80k miles, a rebuilt engine with DPF-compatible calibration offers better uptime and lower total cost.
problems.cta: Enquire about 4P10 Mitsubishi Cantar engine replacement

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure Across Cantar Range - Boost Loss & Oil Consumption
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Cantar FB, FE, FG with turbo diesel (all generations, 4D56/4M40/4M42/4P10)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil seal wear or bearing failure causes oil consumption, blue smoke, and loss of boost pressure under load.

repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if caught early; not for catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new/recon turbo, clean oil/intercooler system
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses contamination; doesn't fix engine wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new turbo, upgraded oil system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for engines with oil contamination or high mileage

problems.recommendation: If your Cantar shows blue smoke or oil loss, check turbo first. For engines over 90k miles with oil contamination, replacement with a rebuilt unit including a new turbo is more reliable than repair.
problems.cta: Compare turbo-ready rebuilt Cantar engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Cantar
finalCta.paragraph: The problems above show that Cantar engine failures often cascade - a timing chain jump damages valves, an EGR leak cracks the head, a fuel pipe leak ruins injectors. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Cantar specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Every unit includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery or supply & fit options.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Cantar Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Cantar and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Cantar Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Cantar is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Cantar 2.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.5L diesel powered early FB light-duty Cantars, offering fuel economy for urban delivery work. Simple mechanical injection made it robust but injector pump wear became common at higher mileage.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Cantar 2.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Cantar 2.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.8L diesel is the Cantar's most common displacement, spanning the 4D56 and 4M40 engine families across FB and FE chassis. High search volume reflects its dominance in UK fleet use and known timing chain/EGR issues.
commonFailurePoints: 4D56 timing chain tensioner wear; 4M40 EGR cooler cracking
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Cantar 2.8L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Cantar 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.0L common-rail diesel powered mid-to-late generation FE and FG Cantars, delivering Euro 4/5 compliance. The 4M42 and 4P10 codes dominate replacement searches due to fuel pipe recalls and injector failures.
commonFailurePoints: 4M42 fuel pipe leak recall; 4P10 common rail injector failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Cantar 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Cantar 3.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.3L diesel served heavier FE applications, offering increased torque for tipper and recovery bodies. Less common than 3.0L units but shares 4M42 architecture and failure patterns.
commonFailurePoints: Cylinder head gasket failure under load
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Cantar 3.3L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Cantar 3.9L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 3.9L diesel powered early FG heavy-duty Cantars, delivering torque for specialist applications. Mechanical injection simplified repairs but fuel system wear drove replacement demand.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Cantar 3.9L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Cantar 4.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 4.2L diesel serves modern FG heavy-duty Cantars, combining common-rail efficiency with heavy-load capability. Higher rebuild costs reflect specialist machining requirements.

years.description: The original FB Cantar launched with the proven 4D56 diesel, establishing the light-duty commercial template.
keyChanges: FB chassis introduced for 2.8-tonne payload class

knownFor: Robust mechanical injection system
Timing chain tensioner wear emerges as key failure

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Cantar Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mitsubishi Cantar engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Cantar engines typically cost £1,450-£4,200 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour.
keyPoints: 4D56 rebuilt engines: £1,450-£2,800 supply-only
4M40/4M42 rebuilt engines: £1,650-£3,600 supply-only
4P10 common rail rebuilt: £2,200-£4,200 supply-only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £450-£850
All prices include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Cantar engine replacement prices now

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Mitsubishi Cantar engine or buying a new vehicle?
items.answer: For Cantars valued under £12,000, engine replacement is usually more economical than purchasing a new commercial vehicle, especially with warranty coverage.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost typically 15-30% of new vehicle price
12-month warranty reduces post-repair risk
Replacement retains familiar chassis and body configuration
Consider ULEZ/CAZ compliance when deciding
Fleet operators often prefer replacement for downtime control
items.cta: Get a tailored quote for your Mitsubishi Cantar engine replacement

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Cantar engines to fail most commonly?
items.answer: The 4D56 timing chain tensioner and 4M40 EGR cooler cracking are the most frequently reported failure causes driving Cantar engine replacement searches.
keyPoints: 4D56: timing chain tensioner wear at 60k-90k miles
4M40: EGR cooler internal cracking causing coolant loss
4M42: fuel pipe recall leak (2007-2010 models)
4P10: common rail injector failure at 60k-90k miles
Turbo oil seal wear affects all generations at higher mileage
items.cta: Find rebuilt Cantar engines with upgraded failure-point components

items.question: How long does it take to fit a replacement engine in a Mitsubishi Cantar?
items.answer: Professional supply & fit for a Mitsubishi Cantar engine typically takes 1-2 working days, depending on variant and workshop availability.
keyPoints: Standard FE 3.5-tonne: 1 full working day typical
Heavy-duty FG variants: 1.5-2 days due to access complexity
Mobile fitting available in many UK regions
Pre-booked appointments minimise commercial downtime
Road test and handover included in professional fitting
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Cantar engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Does a replacement Mitsubishi Cantar engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes - every rebuilt, reconditioned or remanufactured Cantar engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK workshop
Reconditioned and used engines include shorter warranty options
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier
Warranty documentation provided before dispatch

items.question: Will a replacement engine fit my specific Mitsubishi Cantar variant?
items.answer: Using your registration number, we verify exact chassis code, engine code and payload class to ensure the replacement engine is fully compatible with your Cantar.

Engine code match ensures mounting points and ECU compatibility

Body type (tipper, luton, recovery) may affect ancillary fitment
Our specialists confirm compatibility before order confirmation

items.question: Can I fit a Mitsubishi Cantar engine myself to save money?
items.answer: While technically possible, DIY Cantar engine replacement is not recommended due to commercial vehicle weight, specialist tooling requirements and warranty implications.
keyPoints: Cantar engines weigh 250-400kg requiring proper lifting equipment
Electronic management systems require specialist diagnostic tools
Incorrect installation voids supplier warranty coverage
Professional fitting includes calibration, road test and documentation
Supply-only option available if you have qualified workshop access
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Cantar engine supply-only or supply & fit

items.question: How quickly can I get a replacement Mitsubishi Cantar engine delivered?
items.answer: Most rebuilt Mitsubishi Cantar engines are available for next-day or 48-hour delivery within mainland UK, subject to stock and order confirmation.
keyPoints: Common codes (4D56, 4M40, 4M42) often held in stock
Less common codes (4P10, 4M50) may require 3-5 day build time

items.question: Should I choose a used, reconditioned or rebuilt Mitsubishi Cantar engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines offer the best balance of reliability, warranty and value for commercial Cantar operators; used engines suit budget-limited short-term needs.
keyPoints: Used: lowest cost (£850-£2,100) but limited warranty, variable history
Reconditioned: mid-price (£1,450-£3,600), selective part replacement
Rebuilt: comprehensive renewal (£1,650-£4,200), 12-month warranty standard
Commercial uptime priorities favour rebuilt for long-term value
Budget-constrained fleets may use used for non-critical vehicles
